competitor_patterns: Extreme depth: top-ranking posts average 15,000–25,000 words (Bikerumor: 24,883; Outdoorgearlab: 16,529) — they test 10–18 products with granular per-product sections, while our best posts cap at ~8,000 words
Structured comparison tables are universal in top-3 results — every ranking page pairs tables with FAQ schema, enabling rich snippets and AI Overview eligibility simultaneously

H2 architecture follows a strict buyer-journey funnel: Quick Picks → Category winners (MTB / Road / Value / Budget) → How-to-choose guide — giving Google clear topical signals for multiple sub-intents per single URL

Third-party credibility signals dominate: phrases like 'We tested X models', 'Tested & Ranked', and named expert reviewers appear in every title and snippet, directly addressing the E-E-A-T gap that pure brand pages face

Annual freshness is explicit in titles, H1s, and URLs ('2026' appears in title + H1 + URL on every top-3 result), and pages show visible update dates — signalling recency to both crawlers and users scanning SERPs
our_gaps: Word count ceiling: our deepest posts are ~8,100 words vs competitors averaging 16,000–25,000 — we lack the per-product depth (weight data, lens transmittance tables, real-ride scenario paragraphs) that earns topical authority for high-volume head terms
FAQ schema coverage is only 33% across our published pages — 27 posts are missing FAQ schema, directly costing us rich-snippet eligibility on queries where competitors already hold featured snippets

We have no 'tested by us' narrative: competitor snippets lead with 'We tested 18 models' — our posts compare specs but lack a first-person test framework (ride conditions, distances, measurements), which suppresses E-E-A-T signals

Zero MTB-specific or gravel-specific pillar content: Outdoorgearlab ranks #1 partly because it segments by ride type (MTB / Road / Gravel / Value) under one URL — our gravel-vs-road post is only 1,037 words with no table or FAQ schema

Our thin posts (1,015–1,350 words, 27 of 41 pages) have no table AND no FAQ schema simultaneously — these pages contribute almost no SERP equity and dilute crawl budget without consolidation or expansion
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses review — Bing/Tifosi ranks #1; we have posts but none framing Velluto as the definitive interchangeable-lens system with a tool-free click-in test narrative
lightweight cycling sunglasses under 30 grams — no competitor owns this ultra-specific weight angle; Velluto's 25g frame is the sharpest differentiator and this long-tail has clear purchase intent
best cycling glasses for all-day rides — 'long ride comfort' and 'pressure-free fit' are recurring buyer concerns in competitor FAQ sections; we have thin posts on this but no authoritative pillar

anti-fog cycling glasses road cycling 2026 — Intheknow ranks #1 for road cycling glasses review partly via photochromic + anti-fog content; our anti-fog posts are all under 1,260 words with no table/FAQ schema

cycling sunglasses with 30-day trial — no competitor can offer this; zero search coverage exists for this angle, making it a zero-competition brand-term expansion opportunity for AI Overviews and long-tail captures
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 6,000 words for any post addressing a near-top-3 keyword (oakley alternatives pos 6.1, best cycling sunglasses 2026 pos 7.8) — structure with Quick Picks table first, then per-category H2s (Road / Gravel / Value / Budget), then buying guide, then FAQ with ≥6 questions

Open every post with a first-person test claim: specify ride distance, terrain, and date (e.g. 'We rode 847 km across 28 days of summer 2026 testing to rank these') — this directly counters the E-E-A-T gap vs Outdoorgearlab and Bikerumor snippets

Include a comparison table within the first 300 words (above the fold) — GSC shows our table=yes posts outperform table=NO posts on CTR; competitors with tables rank in positions 1–3 while our table-less posts sit at 7–8

Add FAQ schema to every new post without exception — with only 33% FAQ schema coverage site-wide, every new post without it widens the gap; include ≥5 Q&As targeting the exact phrasing of GSC queries (e.g. 'What are the best cycling sunglasses in 2026?', 'Are Oakley alternatives as good?')

For posts targeting 'veluto' (228 impressions, 0.9% CTR, pos 5.7) — this is a misspelling of our brand; create or optimise a page that captures this variant explicitly in meta title and first paragraph without looking spammy (e.g. 'Looking for Velluto cycling glasses? Here's where to start')

Segment every roundup by ride type using H2s: at minimum Road / Gravel / Value — this matches Outdoorgearlab's H2 architecture that earns multiple featured snippet positions and signals breadth of topical authority to Google

Lead with Velluto's three hardest-to-match differentiators in the first 150 words of every post: 25g weight (lightest in category), tool-free interchangeable lenses, 30-day trial — these are the hooks that win clicks from shoppers already comparison-browsing competitors
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema to all 27 posts currently missing it — prioritise the 8 posts that already have tables (table=yes, faq_schema=NO): sigma-vs-velluto, best-cycling-glasses-for-tour-de-france, best-cycling-glasses-for-all-weather, smart-cycling-glasses-worth-it, roka-vs-velluto, poc-cycling-glasses-vs-budget, interchangeable-lens-2026-best-systems-tested, lightweight-cycling-glasses-for-long-rides — these are highest-leverage as they're partially optimised already

Expand all thin posts (under 1,400 words) that target near-top-3 keywords to at least 2,500 words with a comparison table and FAQ schema, or 301-redirect them into the nearest existing pillar post — 17 posts under 1,300 words with no table and no FAQ schema are actively diluting crawl budget and domain authority

Standardise meta title format to '[Primary Keyword] [Year]: [Specific Hook] | Velluto' across all pages — current inconsistency in title structure is suppressing CTR on brand-adjacent queries where position 5–8 titles need a stronger click trigger than competitors at positions 1–3
meta_title_fixes: QUERY 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 7.8, CTR 2.7%) → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested & Ranked | Why: Mirrors exact language of Outdoorgearlab's #1 title ('Tested & Ranked') which users are already clicking; adds credibility signal missing from current title

QUERY 'veluto' (pos 5.7, CTR 0.9%) → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Store 2026 | Why: CTR of 0.9% on 228 impressions means ~226 searchers see us and don't click; a title clarifying this IS the brand (not a review site) with 'Official Store' will capture misspelling-intent navigational clicks — also add 'veluto' as an explicit alt spelling in the meta description

QUERY 'best cycling glasses' (pos 8.2, CTR 2.9%) → New title: Best Cycling Glasses 2026: 25g Ultralight Tested | Velluto | Why: Position 8.2 requires a differentiating hook beyond the generic keyword — '25g Ultralight' is a specific, scannable number that no competitor title contains, creating a pattern interrupt in a SERP full of identical 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' titles
content_quick_wins: QUERY 'velluto' (pos 4.7, 1002 impressions, 48 clicks) → Topic: Velluto StradaPro — Full Brand & Product Hub Page | Add: A dedicated /velluto or homepage-level content section summarising all StradaPro specs (25g, VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione lenses, 30-day trial, anti-fog, UV400, Italian design), structured with FAQ schema answering 'What is Velluto?', 'Where is Velluto made?', 'Is Velluto worth it?' — 1,002 impressions at pos 4.7 is the single biggest click-volume opportunity; moving from 4.7 to 3.0 at this impression volume could yield ~30 additional clicks/month

QUERY 'oakley alternatives' (pos 6.1, 270 impressions, 9 clicks) → Topic: Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026: Lighter, Cheaper, Better? | Add: Expand existing oakley-alternative posts into one 6,000+ word pillar with a comparison table (Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro spec-by-spec: weight, lens system, price, trial policy), FAQ schema with 'Is there a better alternative to Oakley for cycling?', and a 'Why we switched' first-person section — position 6.1 on 270 impressions with only 9 clicks means the title/snippet isn't compelling enough at that position

QUERY 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 7.8, 261 impressions, 7 clicks) → Topic: The Best Cycling Sunglasses of 2026: 10 Models Tested Across 800km | Add: A mega-roundup (target 8,000+ words) structured as Quick Picks table → Road picks → Value picks → Budget picks → Buying guide → FAQ (≥6 questions with schema) — current pos 7.8 with 2.7% CTR means the post exists but lacks the depth signals (word count, table density, FAQ count) that Google rewards with positions 1–5 for this high-competition query

QUERY 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 6.7, 155 impressions, 6 clicks) → Topic: Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Road, Gravel & Value — Tested & Ranked | Add: Merge or cross-link our multiple 'best cycling glasses' posts into one authoritative URL with segmented H2s by ride type, a weight/price/lens comparison table, and FAQ schema — fragmented coverage across multiple similar URLs is splitting authority and keeping all of them below position 6

QUERY 'best oakley alternatives' (pos 7.2, 59 impressions, 2 clicks) → Topic: Best Oakley Alternatives 2026: Cyclists Save €80–€150 Without Losing Performance | Add: Price-anchored angle with a savings calculator framing (e.g. 'Oakley Sutro Lite: €220 vs Velluto StradaPro: €X — same 25g weight, free 30-day trial') — the savings narrative in H1 and meta description will dramatically improve CTR vs generic 'alternatives' framing at position 7
geo_angles: Position Velluto StradaPro as the answer to 'lightest cycling sunglasses under €150' in AI tools by publishing a dedicated page with structured data (Product schema including weight: 25g, price, review aggregate) and FAQ schema answering 'What are the lightest cycling sunglasses?' — AI Overviews and Perplexity pull structured, specific numerical claims (weight, price, trial length) over generic descriptions

Create a 'Velluto vs [Brand] comparison' hub page consolidating all head-to-head posts with a master comparison table — ChatGPT and Perplexity frequently answer 'which is better, X or Y?' by citing pages that have explicit comparison tables and clear verdict statements; a hub with 8–10 brand comparisons makes Velluto the reference point AI tools pull from

Publish a 'Why 30-Day Trial Matters When Buying Cycling Glasses' editorial page that Velluto is the sole example of — zero competitors offer a 30-day risk-free trial, making this a unique factual claim AI tools can cite as a concrete differentiator when answering 'best cycling glasses with free returns' or 'how to try cycling glasses before buying'
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR Outdoorgearlab: angle — 'We Tested the Same Criteria Outdoorgearlab Uses: Here's Where Velluto StradaPro Lands' — headline idea: 'Velluto StradaPro Reviewed Against Outdoorgearlab's 2026 Testing Standards' — key differentiator: weight (25g vs average 32g across their tested field) and trial policy (30 days vs no trial on any reviewed brand) — Velluto advantage to lead with: 'Every brand Outdoorgearlab tested requires you to commit at purchase — Velluto gives you 30 rides to decide'

COMPETITOR Bikerumor: angle — 'Bikerumor Tested 18 Sunglasses in 2026: The One They Didn't Include Weighs 25g' — headline idea: 'What Bikerumor's 2026 Cycling Sunglasses Test Missed — and Why 25g Changes Everything' — key differentiator: Bikerumor's top pick Julbo Density and Oakley Sutro Lite both exceed 28g; Velluto's 25g + interchangeable lens system addresses every criterion in their rubric — Velluto advantage to lead with: ultralight frame weight as a measurable, testable spec rather than a marketing claim

COMPETITOR Tifosi (ranking via Bing for interchangeable lens cycling glasses): angle — 'Tifosi vs Velluto: Both Have Interchangeable Lenses — Only One Has a 30-Day Trial' — headline idea: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro 2026: Lens Systems, Weight & Real-Ride Test Compared' — key differentiator: Tifosi's interchangeable system requires tools or significant force vs Velluto's tool-free click-in VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione swap — Velluto advantage to lead with: 'Mid-ride lens swap in under 10 seconds, no tools, even with gloves on'
